heading: Enhancing tracking and tracing of food and recordkeeping
label: 2223 Enhancing tracking and tracing of food and recordkeeping

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”), taking into account recommendations from the Secretary of Agriculture and representatives of State departments of health and agriculture, shall establish pilot projects in coordination with the food industry to explore and evaluate methods to rapidly and effectively identify recipients of food to prevent or mitigate a foodborne illness outbreak and to address credible threats of serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals as a result of such food being adulterated under or misbranded under .January 4, 2011section 342 of this titlesection 343(w) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: develop and demonstrate methods for rapid and effective tracking and tracing of foods in a manner that is practicable for facilities of varying sizes, including small businesses;

children.label: B
children.text: develop and demonstrate appropriate technologies, including technologies existing on , that enhance the tracking and tracing of food; andJanuary 4, 2011

children.label: C
children.text: inform the promulgation of regulations under subsection (d).

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Secretary shall report to Congress on the findings of the pilot projects under this subsection together with recommendations for improving the tracking and tracing of food.January 4, 2011

children.label: A
children.text: the costs and benefits associated with the adoption and use of several product tracing technologies, including technologies used in the pilot projects under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: the feasibility of such technologies for different sectors of the food industry, including small businesses; and

children.label: C
children.text: whether such technologies are compatible with the requirements of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluate domestic and international product tracing practices in commercial use;

children.label: B
children.text: consider international efforts, including an assessment of whether product tracing requirements developed under this section are compatible with global tracing systems, as appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: consult with a diverse and broad range of experts and stakeholders, including representatives of the food industry, agricultural producers, and nongovernmental organizations that represent the interests of consumers.

children.heading: Product tracing system
children.label: c Product tracing system
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Agriculture, shall, as appropriate, establish within the Food and Drug Administration a product tracing system to receive information that improves the capacity of the Secretary to effectively and rapidly track and trace food that is in the United States or offered for import into the United States. Prior to the establishment of such product tracing system, the Secretary shall examine the results of applicable pilot projects and shall ensure that the activities of such system are adequately supported by the results of such pilot projects.

children.heading: Additional recordkeeping requirements for high risk foods
children.label: d Additional recordkeeping requirements for high risk foods
A
children.text: relate only to information that is reasonably available and appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: be science-based;

children.label: C
children.text: not prescribe specific technologies for the maintenance of records;

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that the public health benefits of imposing additional recordkeeping requirements outweigh the cost of compliance with such requirements;

children.label: E
children.text: be scale-appropriate and practicable for facilities of varying sizes and capabilities with respect to costs and recordkeeping burdens, and not require the creation and maintenance of duplicate records where the information is contained in other company records kept in the normal course of business;

children.label: F
children.text: minimize the number of different recordkeeping requirements for facilities that handle more than 1 type of food;

children.label: G
children.text: to the extent practicable, not require a facility to change business systems to comply with such requirements;

children.label: H
children.text: allow any person subject to this subsection to maintain records required under this subsection at a central or reasonably accessible location provided that such records can be made available to the Secretary not later than 24 hours after the Secretary requests such records; and1So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.1

children.label: I
children.text: include a process by which the Secretary may issue a waiver of the requirements under this subsection if the Secretary determines that such requirements would result in an economic hardship for an individual facility or a type of facility;

children.label: J
children.text: be commensurate with the known safety risks of the designated food;

children.label: K
children.text: take into account international trade obligations;

children.label: i
children.text: a full pedigree, or a record of the complete previous distribution history of the food from the point of origin of such food;

children.label: ii
children.text: records of recipients of a food beyond the immediate subsequent recipient of such food; or

children.label: iii
children.text: product tracking to the case level by persons subject to such requirements; and

children.label: M
children.text: include a process by which the Secretary may remove a high-risk food designation developed under paragraph (2) for a food or type of food.

children.label: i
children.text: the known safety risks of a particular food, including the history and severity of foodborne illness outbreaks attributed to such food, taking into consideration foodborne illness data collected by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention;

children.label: ii
children.text: the likelihood that a particular food has a high potential risk for microbiological or chemical contamination or would support the growth of pathogenic microorganisms due to the nature of the food or the processes used to produce such food;

children.label: iii
children.text: the point in the manufacturing process of the food where contamination is most likely to occur;

children.label: iv
children.text: the likelihood of contamination and steps taken during the manufacturing process to reduce the possibility of contamination;

children.label: v
children.text: the likelihood that consuming a particular food will result in a foodborne illness due to contamination of the food; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the likely or known severity, including health and economic impacts, of a foodborne illness attributed to a particular food.

children.heading: List of high-risk foods
children.label: B List of high-risk foods
children.text: At the time the Secretary promulgates the final rules under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall publish the list of the foods designated under subparagraph (A) as high-risk foods on the Internet website of the Food and Drug Administration. The Secretary may update the list to designate new high-risk foods and to remove foods that are no longer deemed to be high-risk foods, provided that each such update to the list is consistent with the requirements of this subsection and notice of such update is published in the Federal Register.

children.label: A
children.text: prevent unauthorized reproduction of trade secret or confidential information;

children.label: B
children.text: prevent unauthorized access to trade secret or confidential information; and

children.label: C
children.text: maintain records with respect to access by any person to trade secret or confidential information maintained by the agency.

children.heading: Public input
children.label: 4 Public input
children.text: During the comment period in the notice of proposed rulemaking under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall conduct not less than 3 public meetings in diverse geographical areas of the United States to provide persons in different regions an opportunity to comment.

children.heading: Retention of records
children.label: 5 Retention of records
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, the Secretary may require that a facility retain records under this subsection for not more than 2 years, taking into consideration the risk of spoilage, loss of value, or loss of palatability of the applicable food when determining the appropriate timeframes.

children.heading: Farm to school programs
children.label: A Farm to school programs
children.text: In establishing requirements under this subsection, the Secretary shall, in consultation with the Secretary of Agriculture, consider the impact of requirements on farm to school or farm to institution programs of the Department of Agriculture and other farm to school and farm to institution programs outside such agency, and shall modify the requirements under this subsection, as appropriate, with respect to such programs so that the requirements do not place undue burdens on farm to school or farm to institution programs.

children.heading: Identity-preserved labels with respect to farm sales of food that is produced and packaged on a farm
children.label: B Identity-preserved labels with respect to farm sales of food that is produced and packaged on a farm
i
children.text: the packaging of the food maintains the integrity of the product and prevents subsequent contamination or alteration of the product; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the labeling of the food includes the name, complete address (street address, town, State, country, and zip or other postal code), and business phone number of the farm, unless the Secretary waives the requirement to include a business phone number of the farm, as appropriate, in order to accommodate a religious belief of the individual in charge of such farm.

children.heading: Fishing vessels
children.label: C Fishing vessels
children.text: The requirements under this subsection with respect to a food that is produced through the use of a fishing vessel (as defined in ) shall be limited to the requirements under subparagraph (F) until such time as the food is sold by the owner, operator, or agent in charge of such fishing vessel.section 1802(18) of title 16

children.heading: Limitation on extent of tracing
children.label: i Limitation on extent of tracing
children.text: Recordkeeping requirements under this subsection with regard to any commingled raw agricultural commodity shall be limited to the requirements under subparagraph (F).

children.label: I
children.text: the term “commingled raw agricultural commodity” means any commodity that is combined or mixed after harvesting, but before processing;

children.label: II
children.text: the term “commingled raw agricultural commodity” shall not include types of fruits and vegetables that are raw agricultural commodities for which the Secretary has determined that standards promulgated under (as added by section 105) would minimize the risk of serious adverse health consequences or death; andsection 350h of this title2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: III
children.text: the term “processing” means operations that alter the general state of the commodity, such as canning, cooking, freezing, dehydration, milling, grinding, pasteurization, or homogenization.

children.heading: Exemption of other foods
children.label: E Exemption of other foods
children.text: The Secretary may, by notice in the Federal Register, modify the requirements under this subsection with respect to, or exempt a food or a type of facility from, the requirements of this subsection (other than the requirements under subparagraph (F), if applicable) if the Secretary determines that product tracing requirements for such food (such as bulk or commingled ingredients that are intended to be processed to destroy pathogens) or type of facility is not necessary to protect the public health.

children.heading: Recordkeeping regarding previous sources and subsequent recipients
children.label: F Recordkeeping regarding previous sources and subsequent recipients
children.text: In the case of a person or food to which a limitation or exemption under subparagraph (C), (D), or (E) applies, if such person, or a person who manufactures, processes, packs, or holds such food, is required to register with the Secretary under with respect to the manufacturing, processing, packing, or holding of the applicable food, the Secretary shall require such person to maintain records that identify the immediate previous source of such food and the immediate subsequent recipient of such food.section 350d of this title

children.heading: Grocery stores
children.label: G Grocery stores
children.text: With respect to a sale of a food described in subparagraph (H) to a grocery store, the Secretary shall not require such grocery store to maintain records under this subsection other than records documenting the farm that was the source of such food. The Secretary shall not require that such records be kept for more than 180 days.

children.heading: Farm sales to consumers
children.label: H Farm sales to consumers
children.text: The Secretary shall not require a farm to maintain any distribution records under this subsection with respect to a sale of a food described in subparagraph (I) (including a sale of a food that is produced and packaged on such farm), if such sale is made by the farm directly to a consumer.

children.label: i
children.text: the food is produced on a farm; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the sale is made by the owner, operator, or agent in charge of such farm directly to a consumer or grocery store.

children.heading: No impact on non-high-risk foods
children.label: 7 No impact on non-high-risk foods
children.text: The recordkeeping requirements established under paragraph (1) shall have no effect on foods that are not designated by the Secretary under paragraph (2) as high-risk foods. Foods described in the preceding sentence shall be subject solely to the recordkeeping requirements under and subpart J of part 1 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).section 350c of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the product tracing requirements under subsection (d) to foods identified under paragraph (2) of such subsection, including whether such requirements provide adequate assurance of traceability in the event of intentional adulteration, including by acts of terrorism; and

children.label: B
children.text: the participation of restaurants in the recordkeeping requirements.

children.heading: Determination and recommendations
children.label: 2 Determination and recommendations
children.text: In conducting the evaluation and report under paragraph (1), if the Comptroller General of the United States determines that the limitations described in such paragraph do not adequately protect the public health, the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress recommendations, if appropriate, regarding recordkeeping requirements for restaurants and additional foods, in order to protect the public health.

children.label: A
children.text: is adulterated under ;section 342 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: presents a threat of serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals; and

children.label: C
children.text: was adulterated as described in subparagraph (A) on a particular farm (as defined in section 1.227 of chapter 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation)).3So in original. Probably should be “title”.3

children.heading: Manner of request
children.label: 2 Manner of request
children.text: In making a request under paragraph (1), the Secretary, in consultation and coordination with State and local agencies responsible for food safety, as appropriate, shall issue a written notice to the owner, operator, or agent of the farm to which the article of food has been traced. The individual providing such notice shall present to such owner, operator, or agent appropriate credentials and shall deliver such notice at reasonable times and within reasonable limits and in a reasonable manner.

children.heading: Delivery of information requested
children.label: 3 Delivery of information requested
children.text: The owner, operator, or agent of a farm shall deliver the information requested under paragraph (1) in a prompt and reasonable manner. Such information may consist of records kept in the normal course of business, and may be in electronic or non-electronic format.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: A request made under paragraph (1) shall not include a request for information relating to the finances, pricing of commodities produced, personnel, research, sales (other than information relating to shipping), or other disclosures that may reveal trade secrets or confidential information from the farm to which the article of food has been traced, other than information necessary to identify potential immediate recipients of such food. and the Freedom of Information Act [] shall apply with respect to any confidential commercial information that is disclosed to the Food and Drug Administration in the course of responding to a request under paragraph (1).Section 331(j) of this title5 U.S.C. 552

children.heading: Records
children.label: 5 Records
children.text: Except with respect to identifying potential immediate recipients in response to a request under this subsection, nothing in this subsection shall require the establishment or maintenance by farms of new records.

children.heading: No Limitation on commingling of food
children.label: g No Limitation on commingling of food
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize the Secretary to impose any limitation on the commingling of food.

children.heading: Small entity compliance guide
children.label: h Small entity compliance guide
children.text: Not later than 180 days after promulgation of a final rule under subsection (d), the Secretary shall issue a small entity compliance guide setting forth in plain language the requirements of the regulations under such subsection in order to assist small entities, including farms and small businesses, in complying with the recordkeeping requirements under such subsection.

to small businesses (as defined by the Secretary in , not later than 90 days after ) beginning on the date that is 1 year after the effective date of the final regulations promulgated under subsection (d); andsection 350g of this titleJanuary 4, 2011

to very small businesses (as defined by the Secretary in , not later than 90 days after ) beginning on the date that is 2 years after the effective date of the final regulations promulgated under subsection (d).section 350g of this titleJanuary 4, 2011
